summary: Co-hosts playing an old-school 442/424, pressing high and built around Jonathan David as the link. Real hope they can ride the home support.
style: Old-school 4-4-2 / 4-2-4 — wingers high & wide, a fullback inverting to help the CMs, Jonathan David dropping in as the link, high press initially then recover and repeat.

counterReliance.note: High press to win it high, then recover & repeat

strengths: High initial press to win the ball up the pitch
Jonathan David as a high-class link forward
Exciting, talented squad with home-nation momentum

keyPlayers.name: Jonathan David
keyPlayers.note: Pacey, powerful & technical — 39 international goals, 150+ club goals; comes short to link play

tactics: Wingers stay high and wide while an inverting fullback supports the midfield. David drops to link and the press is aggressive early before recovering shape. Host-nation bias acknowledged, but the talent and atmosphere could build real momentum.
sources: John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
